articles.title: Vedānta as a realist tradition — a dialectical history in the schools' own voices
articles.subtitle: Pre-Śaṅkara through early-modern Vedānta as a chain of named polemics rather than a museum of labels: Bhartṛprapañca behind Śaṅkara, Maṇḍana and Sureśvara, the Padmapāda–Vācaspati–Prakāśātman quarrels over the locus of avidyā, Rāmānuja's *saptavidhānupapatti*, Madhva and Vyāsatīrtha forcing Advaita into formal self-defence, Madhusūdana's reply, the Gauḍīya *acintya-bhedābheda* reformulation, and Trika's structural convergence with Madhusūdana.

articles.title: Śaṅkara — *adhyāsa*, *mithyā* without *asat*, the *līlā-kaivalyam* of the Self
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Brahmasūtra-bhāṣya*, *Upadeśasāhasrī*, and the major Upaniṣad commentaries that holds *mithyā* and *asat* sharply apart, recovers the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya*'s precise epistemology of superimposition, and reads liberation as the play-already-of-the-Self rather than a world-cancellation.

articles.title: Rāmānuja — *saptavidhānupapatti*, *apṛthak-siddhi*, and the body of Brahman
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Śrī-bhāṣya* and *Vedārtha-saṃgraha* tracking the seven untenabilities against indeterminable avidyā, the doctrine of inseparable existence, and the qualified-non-dual architecture in which selves and world are real modes of the body of Brahman.

articles.title: Madhva — *pañcabheda*, *viśeṣa*, and the *māyāvāda* critique
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Brahmasūtra-bhāṣya*, *Anuvyākhyāna*, *Viṣṇu-tattva-vinirṇaya*, and *Māyāvāda-khaṇḍana* that recovers the fivefold real difference, the technical role of *viśeṣa* in distinguishing-without-separating, and Madhva's quotation-and-refutation of the Advaitin's own slogans — without collapsing his realism into crude dualism.

articles.title: Caitanya, Jīva Gosvāmī, Baladeva — *acintya-bhedābheda* as formal logic
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the Gauḍīya *Sandarbhas* and the *Govinda-bhāṣya* showing how *acintya-śakti* is not a hand-wave but a precise device for holding simultaneous identity and difference, with the *bhakti-rasa* doctrine and the rasa-theological architecture read structurally rather than devotionally.

articles.title: Sri Aurobindo — triple-status Supermind, willful self-veiling, the gnostic being
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *The Life Divine*, *The Synthesis of Yoga*, *The Secret of the Veda*, and the *Record of Yoga* that recovers the triple status of Supermind (comprehending–apprehending–projecting), the doctrine of willful divine self-ignorance, and the architecture of the descending gnostic being — without folding it into vague mysticism.

articles.title: Chaudhuri and Banerji — what is already in print since 1967
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through Haridas Chaudhuri (*Integral Yoga*, 1965; *The Evolution of Integral Consciousness*, 1977) and Debashish Banerji (*Seven Quartets of Becoming*, 2020; *The Alternate Nation of Abanindranath Tagore*; the *Monsoon* essays) showing where the contemporary Aurobindonian literature already articulates much of the user's territory — the Spirit/Matter play, the Supermind ≈ rhizome equation, and *māyā* as the *śakti of vijñāna*.

articles.title: Krishnachandra Bhattacharyya — *The Subject as Freedom*, alternative absolutes, *Swaraj in Ideas*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *The Subject as Freedom* (1930), *Studies in Vedantism* (1909), the two volumes of *Studies in Philosophy*, and the 1928 "Swaraj in Ideas" lecture, locating KCB's doctrine of subjecthood-as-freedom and his three alternative absolutes — Truth, Freedom, Value — and naming where the architecture stops short of the user's own.

articles.title: Ramakrishna and Vivekananda — *vijñāna* doctrine, Bengali Vedānta, the Mother
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Kathāmṛta*, the *Complete Works* of Vivekananda, and the Holy Mother's letters, recovering the *vijñāna* register beyond *jñāna*, the *adhikāra-bheda* logic of Ramakrishna's hierarchy as pedagogical rather than metaphysical, and the bridge between Bengali devotional realism and modern Advaita.

articles.title: Swami Medhananda — *Vijñāna Vedānta* and Vivekananda's cosmopolitanism
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Infinite Paths to Infinite Reality* (OUP 2018) and *Swami Vivekananda's Vedāntic Cosmopolitanism* (OUP 2022), with the *Religions* / *Monist* / *Philosophy East and West* / *Journal of Religion* essays, locating the strongest current academic articulation of the Bhartṛprapañca–Bhāskara–Madhva–Caitanya–Aurobindo realist trajectory and naming where the user's project extends past it.

articles.title: Reading Sāṃkhya with Anirban Bandyopadhyay — three-guṇa metaphysics and contemporary biophysics
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through Īśvarakṛṣṇa's *Sāṃkhya-Kārikā* with Gauḍapāda and Vācaspati Miśra, the structural twin in the *Yoga-Sūtras* with Vyāsa's *Bhāṣya*, read in counterpoint with Anirban Bandyopadhyay's Phase Prime Metric formalisation of the three guṇas — and a connection back through Deleuze on seed, tree, and rhizome.

articles.title: Kāla-Cakra and nested topological clock-structures
articles.subtitle: A reading-session that places Anirban Bandyopadhyay's polyatomic-time-crystal architecture against the classical Indic Kāla-Cakra: Bhāgavata cosmology, the four pralayas, the *Kālacakra-Tantra*, and the metaphysics of nested temporal recurrence in Sanskrit Buddhist and Hindu canon.

articles.title: Deleuze — univocity of being, the rhizomatic real, and the fold
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in original French with English translation through *Différence et Répétition*, *Logique du sens*, *Mille Plateaux*, *Le Pli*, and *Spinoza et le problème de l'expression* — the Scotist heritage, the violence of unification, and what the apparatus enables and where it must be supplemented.

articles.title: Nietzsche — perspectivism, eternal return, the genealogy of values
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Die Geburt der Tragödie*, *Zur Genealogie der Moral*, *Also sprach Zarathustra*, *Jenseits von Gut und Böse*, and the *Nachlass* — recovering perspectivism as a discipline of reading and the eternal return as a thought-experiment of affirmation rather than a cosmology.

articles.title: Hegel — *Aufhebung*, the speculative proposition, the labor of the negative
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Phänomenologie des Geistes* (Preface and Introduction in particular), the *Wissenschaft der Logik*, and the *Encyclopädie*, recovering *Aufhebung* as triple-action, the speculative proposition as a non-predicative form, and *Substanz als Subjekt* without smoothing it into easy organicism.

articles.title: Spinoza — substance, attribute, mode; *intellectus infinitus* and the *amor Dei intellectualis*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in Latin with English translation through the *Ethica*, the *Tractatus de Intellectus Emendatione*, and the *Korte Verhandeling* recovering the geometric architecture, the parallelism of attributes, and the *amor Dei intellectualis* as the *quinta pars*'s consummation.

articles.title: Leibniz — the monad as perspective, pre-established harmony, the aperspectival God
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Monadologie*, *Discours de métaphysique*, *Nouveaux Essais*, and the late correspondence with Clarke and Des Bosses, recovering the monad as point-of-view, the architecture of compossibility, and Leibniz's anticipation of a perspectivism whose limit is the divine view that holds all perspectives at once.

articles.title: Heidegger — *Sein und Zeit*, *Ereignis*, the clearing
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in German with English through *Sein und Zeit*, the *Wegmarken* essays (*Brief über den Humanismus*, *Vom Wesen der Wahrheit*, *Was ist Metaphysik?*), *Identität und Differenz*, the *Beiträge*, and *Gelassenheit* — keeping the German first where the standard translations smooth Heidegger toward the metaphysics he was trying to break.

articles.title: Husserl — intentionality, the *Lebenswelt*, transcendental constitution
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in German through the *Logische Untersuchungen*, the *Ideen*, the *Cartesianische Meditationen*, and the *Krisis*, following Husserl's own self-description of phenomenology as one continuous deepening — from the founding studies through the meditational radicalization to the late return to *Lebenswelt* and pre-predicative experience.

articles.title: Adorno — *Negative Dialektik*, identity-thinking as violence, the *Vorrang des Objekts*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in German through *Negative Dialektik*, *Minima Moralia*, *Dialektik der Aufklärung* (with Horkheimer), and *Jargon der Eigentlichkeit* — testing whether Adorno's polemic against Hegelian totalization confirms the user's positive metaphysics or sets the sharpest barrier to it.

articles.title: Bergson — *durée*, the *élan vital*, intuition as method
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in French through *Essai sur les données immédiates de la conscience*, *Matière et mémoire*, *L'évolution créatrice*, and *Les deux sources de la morale et de la religion*, recovering *durée* as ontologically primary and intuition as a discipline rather than a faculty — the most direct French anticipation of Aurobindo's intuitive-mental register and the bridge to Whitehead and Deleuze.

articles.title: Whitehead — process, prehension, the dipolar God
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Process and Reality*, *Adventures of Ideas*, *Modes of Thought*, and *Religion in the Making*, recovering the categoreal scheme, the doctrine of actual occasions and prehension, and the two natures of God — primordial and consequent — as the strongest Western articulation of being-as-becoming.

articles.title: Prigogine — irreversibility, dissipative structures, the arrow of time
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *La nouvelle alliance* (with Stengers), *From Being to Becoming*, *The End of Certainty*, and the technical papers on far-from-equilibrium thermodynamics — Whitehead in physics, supplying empirical traction for what was already claimed at the level of metaphysics.

articles.title: Derrida — *différance*, trace, deconstruction
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *De la grammatologie*, *L'écriture et la différence*, *Marges de la philosophie*, and *La voix et le phénomène*, holding apart the register at which the user agrees with Derrida (the structure of language) from the register at which he must be rejected (the structure of being) — so the agreement does not collapse into capitulation and the rejection does not collapse into dismissal.

articles.title: Foucault — *épistémè*, power-knowledge, the care of the self
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in French following Foucault's own self-periodization in three modes of objectification — the archaeological *savoirs* (*Folie et déraison*, *Les mots et les choses*, *L'archéologie du savoir*), the genealogical apparatus of power (*Surveiller et punir*, *La volonté de savoir*), and the ethical axis (*L'usage des plaisirs*, *Le souci de soi*, *L'herméneutique du sujet*).

articles.title: Levinas — the face of the Other, *Totalité et Infini*, *Autrement qu'être*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in French through *De l'existence à l'existant*, *Le temps et l'autre*, *Totalité et infini*, *Autrement qu'être ou au-delà de l'essence*, *De Dieu qui vient à l'idée*, and the *Lectures talmudiques* — keeping the *height* of the Other and the violence of *substitution* sharp where the standard English smooths them toward peer-symmetry.

articles.title: McGilchrist — the hemispheric thesis, *non-duality of duality and non-duality*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *The Master and His Emissary* (2009) and *The Matter with Things* (2021), recovering the hemispheric architecture as a substantive metaphysical proposal rather than a popular-neuroscience trope, and the convergence with the Indic claim that the privileged state is the diaphanous through-all of the registers.

articles.title: Gebser — *Ursprung und Gegenwart*, the aperspectival-integral mutation, *Diaphaneität*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Ursprung und Gegenwart* (1949 / 1953 / 1966) and the Barstad–Mickunas English (Ohio UP 1985), recovering the archaic, magic, mythical, mental, and aperspectival-integral structures, the doctrine of *Diaphaneität* as transparency-of-prior-structures rather than higher-perspective, and Gebser's 1966 explicit equation of the integral mutation with Aurobindo's supramental.

articles.title: Hegel's Preface to the *Phänomenologie* — and the Vedāntic parallels
articles.subtitle: *Substanz als Subjekt*, *Aufhebung*, the speculative proposition placed against Bhartṛprapañca, Madhva, Vyāsatīrtha, Jīva Gosvāmī, Vidyāraṇya — a comparative engagement that locates structural parallels without collapsing the disagreements.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā worked — the symbol-environment thesis and the contemporary reader's encounter
articles.subtitle: Reinstates v2's symbolism-shift thesis cleanly, in its proper register, while retaining v3's correct insistence on Mīmāṃsā's coherence on its own terms. The school worked for the practitioners who inhabited the original symbol-environment; the symbol-environment changed across centuries as a structural cultural-historical fact (*Religionsgeschichte*, not moral decline); for participants outside the original substrate, the rite's operations remain analysable on the school's own terms but no longer carry their original immediate spiritual-symbolic register; Aurobindo's hermeneutic is amplification of what was once lived for the contemporary reader, not correction of a defective school. §5 explicitly rejects the school-internal decline narrative (Brahmin corruption, Sanskrit-fluency decay, *kali-yuga*) as a misdiagnosis of structural-cultural change as moral failure.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as a coherent practice-philosophy — Clooney's reading and the contemporary reader's encounter (superseded)
articles.subtitle: Replaces v2's diagnostic-decline framing. The classical Mīmāṃsakas externalised the rite because the symbol-environment they inhabited carried the depth in lived practice; the school is coherent on its own terms. Superseded by v4, which reinstates the symbolism-shift thesis as a structural-cultural fact rather than a moral or school-internal decline narrative.
articles.superseded_note: v3 over-corrected v2's diagnostic-decline framing by retracting the symbolism-shift thesis entirely. v4 reinstates the symbolism-shift thesis as a structural-cultural fact, not a moral or school-internal decline narrative.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as externalized yogic process — re-investigation defending the Integral-Yoga thesis (superseded)
articles.subtitle: Re-do with stronger verdict, drawing on Clooney 2022 and Śabara on MS 1.1.2; introduces a diagnostic-decline framing that v3 retracts and v4 reformulates. Superseded by v4.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as externalized yogic process — first investigation (superseded)
articles.subtitle: First-pass investigation of three precisifications of the hypothesis that Pūrva-Mīmāṃsā ritual is the externalized form of an inner Vedic yogic process Aurobindo decodes in *The Secret of the Veda*. Superseded by v4.

articles.title: An analytic-primitive model of Vedāntic and adjacent positions
articles.subtitle: Nine structural primitives — substrate-independence, mode-status, identity-relation, causation, locus-of-avidyā, *bheda*-count, epistemic-authority, *mokṣa*-state, *bhakti*-role — and the derivation table per school.

articles.title: The comparative-claims framework — methodology
articles.subtitle: A four-verdict schema for cross-school comparison: shared-presupposition, parallel-structure, terminological-equivalence, genuine-disagreement, contested. Five claim categories. Anti-pattern guards against the standard collapses.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* with Tattva-Prakāśikā, Tātparya-Candrikā, Nyāya-Sudhā, Parimaḷa
articles.subtitle: A walk-through of Madhva's BSB across three commentarial layers — the *bheda-pañcaka* of Tattvavāda, the realism-recovery move on BS 1.1.1, the constructive thesis that *mithyā ≠ asat*.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhva's *Bhāgavata-Tātparya-Nirṇaya*
articles.subtitle: Madhva's settling-of-purport reading of the *Bhāgavata Purāṇa* — the *bhakti* axis of Tattvavāda, the gradation of *jīva*s, the locus of Viṣṇu's primacy in scripture.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhva's *Māyāvāda-Khaṇḍana*
articles.subtitle: The polemical demolition of Advaitic *māyāvāda* — Madhva's argument that *mithyā* construed as ontological non-existence is incoherent, and the constructive corollary for the realism-recovery trajectory.

articles.title: Engagement: Nimbārka's *Vedānta-Pārijāta-Saurabha* with Śrīnivāsa's *Vedānta-Kaustubha*
articles.subtitle: Dvaitādvaita's parallel-realism reading of the Brahma-Sūtras — *bheda* and *abheda* as equiprimordial, the cit–acit–Brahman triad, and where Nimbārka sits between Rāmānuja and Madhva.

articles.title: Engagement: Vallabha's *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the Brahma-Sūtras
articles.subtitle: Śuddhādvaita as a third realism-Vedānta — the world as Brahman's *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva* (manifestation–veiling), the rejection of *pariṇāma* and *vivarta*, and the *bhakti*-axis of pure non-duality.

articles.title: Engagement: Vedānta-Deśika's *Pāñcarātra-Rakṣā*
articles.subtitle: Deśika's defence of the scriptural authority of the Pāñcarātra against Mīmāṃsaka and Smārta attack — the tantric-Vaiṣṇava locus of Viśiṣṭādvaita's ritual ontology.

articles.title: Engagement: Rāghavendra's *Nyāya-Sudhā-Parimaḷa* (4 vols)
articles.subtitle: Rāghavendra's gloss on Jayatīrtha's *Nyāya-Sudhā* on Madhva's *Anuvyākhyāna* — the late-Mādhva polemic apparatus deployed across all four *adhyāya*s of the Brahma-Sūtra.

articles.title: Engagement: Rāghavendra's *Tantra-Dīpikā*
articles.subtitle: Rāghavendra's commentary on Madhva's *Tantra-Sāra-Saṅgraha* — the Mādhva ritual-philosophical interface, mantra ontology, and the *bhakti*–*jñāna* integration.

articles.title: Engagement: Vidyāraṇya's *Vivaraṇa-Prameya-Saṅgraha*
articles.subtitle: The Vivaraṇa-school digest — *śravaṇa* as ascertainment of textual purport, *manana*, *nididhyāsana*, the *mānasī-vṛtti* whose object is *pratyag-brahmaikya*, and the locus of Brahman as both *āśraya* and *viṣaya* of avidyā.

articles.title: Engagement: Vijñānabhikṣu's *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* on the Brahma-Sūtras
articles.subtitle: Vijñānabhikṣu's *avibhāgādvaita* — non-duality-by-non-difference rather than identity, the synthesis of Sāṃkhya and Vedānta, and the late-medieval reframing of *jīva*–Brahman relation.

articles.title: Engagement: Vijñānabhikṣu's *Yoga-Vārttika*
articles.subtitle: Vijñānabhikṣu's gloss on Vyāsa's *Yoga-Bhāṣya* — the practice-philosophy of Pātañjala Yoga read into Vedāntic ontology, the place of Īśvara, the *kleśa*–*vāsanā* architecture.

articles.title: Engagement: Yāmuna's *Āgama-Prāmāṇya*
articles.subtitle: Yāmuna's defence of the Pāñcarātra Āgama as scripturally authoritative — the founding text of the Viśiṣṭādvaita line, refuting the Mīmāṃsaka rejection of non-Vedic ritual literature.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhusūdana Sarasvatī's *Bhakti-Rasāyana*
articles.subtitle: Madhusūdana's reconciliation of *bhakti* with Advaitic non-dualism — *bhakti* as a *rasa* and a distinct *puruṣārtha*, the relation to *Bhāgavata*, and the bridge from *Advaita-Siddhi* to a non-dual devotionalism.

articles.title: Engagement: Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya*
articles.subtitle: The full Viśiṣṭādvaita reading of the Brahma-Sūtras — the *mahā-pūrva-pakṣa* against Advaita on BS 1.1.1, the *aprākṛta-śarīraka* construal of *jīva*–Brahman, and the architecture of qualified non-duality.
